phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — element-brush ambush bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your element-brush ambush bursts beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — element-brush ambush bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your element-brush ambush bursts beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Supreme Display online — any fight near a wall is a stun lottery you rigged. Hug terrain like rent depends on it.

phases.why: W element economy begins: ice for the trades, rock for the executes, grass for the angles.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E joins: the stick-dash into ice-Q root is your first real kill pattern — pick one window per wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Element-cycle the trades: E-ice-Q roots, auto, rock-Q the execute line. The lane is a terrain quiz.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Supreme Display online — any fight near a wall is a stun lottery you rigged. Hug terrain like rent depends on it.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the double-blade spirit dives out-tick your element-brush ambush bursts in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Qiyana flanks through her own R: shockwave the objective wall, string the stunned, and vanish into grass.
